evidence: The amber probe arrow is the clear single focus in each frame, with the periwinkle candidates and lilac score bars playing supporting roles.
The scene holds one probe, four candidates, and their score bars as a single coherent system, not five unrelated novel ideas.
Labels 'probe', c1–c4 sit on the arrows and directly above each score bar; there is no legend or corner key.
Only the instruction line and object labels/readouts appear, each annotating a visible element.
Labels, the probe angle readout, and the numeric dot-product readouts are all legible at every viewport including 375px.
All arrows, bars, and readouts sit fully within the frame with margins at 375, 768, and 1280.
The 'probe' text and angle readout sit beside the probe shaft and remain readable without illegible overlap of other elements.
The probe is consistently amber, candidates periwinkle, score bars lilac, and labels cream throughout all states.
Every element—probe, candidates, bars, readouts, baseline—carries propositional content; no ornaments present.

Initial shows probe at 90°, aligned shows probe on c1 at 20° with c1 bar peaking at +100, orthogonal shows probe at 110° on c3 with c1 at 0 and c3 peak, anti-aligned shows probe at 200° with c1 at -100 below the baseline.
Each score bar and numeric readout sits directly under its labeled candidate, and the probe readout touches the probe.
Across the driven states the probe rotation and bar heights change continuously, reading as a morph rather than a cut.
The composition keeps clear margins and separates the vector dial from the score bars with ample negative space.
